name: IronMind Eval 100
description: Original multiple-choice regression suite for local models: physics, mathematics, and defensive security.

items.question: A car starts from rest and accelerates at 3 m/s^2 for 4 s. How far does it travel?
What is the kinetic energy of a 2 kg object moving at 5 m/s?
A wave has frequency 50 Hz. What is its period?
If the frequency of a photon is doubled, what happens to its energy?
For an ideal gas at constant volume, absolute temperature doubles. What happens to pressure?
A 6 ohm resistor carries 3 A. What voltage is across it?
Two identical capacitors of capacitance C are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent capacitance?
Which quantity is conserved in an isolated collision system?
What is the centripetal acceleration of uniform circular motion with speed v and radius r?
A sample has half-life 10 days. What fraction remains after 30 days?
Neglecting air resistance near Earth, how does free-fall acceleration depend on mass?
A lens with positive focal length is normally classified as which type?
A device does 100 J of work in 5 s. What is its average power?
A sound wave travels at 340 m/s with frequency 170 Hz. What is its wavelength?
As a blackbody temperature increases, the peak wavelength of emitted radiation generally becomes what?
What magnetic force acts on a stationary electric charge in a magnetic field?
Near Earth's surface, gravitational potential energy for mass m at height h is approximately what?
For an ideal gas, molecular RMS speed is proportional to which temperature expression?
A 4 ohm and a 6 ohm resistor are connected in series. What is the equivalent resistance?
A force is exactly perpendicular to displacement. What work does that force do?
Angular momentum is conserved when which condition holds?
Isotopes of the same element have the same number of what?
A system receives 50 J of heat and does 20 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in internal energy?
A light ray passes from air into glass. If it is not normal to the surface, it bends generally in which direction?
Why cannot ordinary sound travel through a vacuum?
What is the escape speed from a spherical body of mass M and radius R?
The de Broglie wavelength of a particle is given by which expression?
For an isolated system, entropy in a spontaneous process generally does what?
Two identical springs of constant k are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent spring constant?
Archimedes' principle says buoyant force equals the weight of what?
What is the time constant of a simple RC circuit?
Which is a valid SI unit for electric field strength?
Pressure is defined as what?
The magnitude of torque from a force F applied at distance r and angle theta is what?
What is the derivative of x^3 with respect to x?
What is the integral of 2x from x=0 to x=3?
Solve 2x + 5 = 17.
What is the determinant of the matrix [[1,2],[3,4]]?
For any real x, sin^2(x) + cos^2(x) equals what?
What is the probability of getting two heads from two fair coin flips?
What is the mean of 2, 4, and 9?
What is the infinite sum 1 + 1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 + ... ?
What is log base 10 of 1000?
What is the area of a circle with radius 3?
What is the derivative of ln(x) for x > 0?
What are the roots of x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0?
What is the slope of the line through (1,2) and (3,6)?
Multiplying a vector by an identity matrix does what?
What is the greatest common divisor of 24 and 36?
What is the least common multiple of 4 and 6?
What is 5 factorial?
In complex arithmetic, what is i^2?
If f(x)=2x, what is f(f(3))?
A data set has standard deviation zero. What must be true?
If events A and B are disjoint, P(A union B) equals what?
The gradient of a scalar function points in the direction of what?
What is 2^10?
What is 17 mod 5?
For two non-vertical perpendicular lines, the product of their slopes is what?
What is the median of the set {1, 7, 3}?
For positive x, e^(ln 7) equals what?
What is the derivative of sin(x)?
How many ways are there to choose 2 objects from 5 distinct objects?
Solve x/4 = 7.
The dot product of two nonzero orthogonal vectors is what?
What is the sum of interior angles of a Euclidean triangle?
If square matrix A is invertible, the system Ax=b has what kind of solution for every b?
Which coding practice most directly prevents SQL injection in normal database queries?
Which is best for storing user passwords?

choices.B: Reversible encryption with the app key
choices.C: A slow salted password hash such as Argon2 or bcrypt

items.question: The principle of least privilege means granting what?

choices.B: Only permissions needed for the task

items.question: Which defense is most relevant when displaying untrusted user text in HTML?

choices.A: Output encoding for the correct context

items.question: A C program copies untrusted input into a fixed-size stack buffer with strcpy. What is the main risk?
Which mechanism is commonly used to protect state-changing web requests from CSRF?

choices.A: CSRF token bound to the session

items.question: Multi-factor authentication mainly reduces the impact of which event?
Secret token comparisons should use constant-time comparison mainly to reduce what risk?
Where should production API secrets generally be kept?

choices.C: In a secret manager or protected environment configuration

items.question: TLS primarily protects data against what while it travels over the network?

choices.A: Unauthorized read or tampering in transit

items.question: Hiding an admin button in the UI is insufficient unless what is also enforced?
Logging access tokens and passwords creates which primary risk?
Why pin and update third-party dependencies?

choices.A: To make builds and vulnerability management more predictable
choices.C: To remove the need for backups

items.question: A permissive CORS policy can be dangerous when combined with what?
An upload handler accepts paths containing ../ without normalization or root enforcement. What issue is likely?
Server-side request forgery risk is reduced by which control?

choices.A: Restricting outbound destinations and blocking metadata/internal ranges

items.question: Building a shell command by concatenating untrusted input can cause what?
Which control helps slow online password guessing?
Fail closed means what should happen when an authorization check cannot complete?
When accepting a signed token, which checks are essential?
Argon2 and bcrypt are designed to be useful for which purpose?
What is a correct statement about static analysis security tools?

choices.A: They can find some patterns but do not prove software is secure

items.question: A threat model should identify which set of things?

choices.A: Assets, trust boundaries, adversaries, and likely attack paths

items.question: For JWT-like tokens, why is verifying the signature important?

choices.A: It checks the token was issued by a trusted signer and not modified

items.question: Zero trust architecture emphasizes which assumption?

choices.A: Every request should be authenticated and authorized

items.question: Memory-safe languages most directly reduce which class of bugs?
For user file uploads, which is a safer design choice?

choices.A: Store outside the web root and validate type/size

items.question: In a database API, prepared statements separate query structure from what?
Content Security Policy is mainly a mitigation for which browser-side risk?
If a production signing key is confirmed compromised, what should happen?

choices.A: Rotate/revoke it and invalidate affected tokens where possible
choices.C: Ignore it until the next release

items.question: Why should backups be regularly restored in tests?
A program checks a file path, then later opens it after an attacker can swap it. What bug pattern is this?
After a user logs in, regenerating the session identifier helps prevent what?
